title: Maintenance System Overhaul
goal: Modernize and standardize the maintenance system by fixing integration branch awareness, removing temporary stop-gaps, establishing proper configuration patterns, and creating a unified maintenance framework that scales with the team's workflow.

milestones.title: Fix Integration Branch Awareness
milestones.description: Update all maintenance tasks to use the configured integration branch (dev) instead of hardcoded main/master checks. This fixes the worktree cleanup bug and standardizes branch detection across the system.

phases.name: update-maintenance-tasks-integration-branch-detection
phases.title: Update maintenance tasks integration branch detection
phases.description: Modify maintenance-tasks.ts to use gitWorkflow.prBaseBranch from settings instead of hardcoded 'main'/'master'. Update worktreeAutoCleanup() and mergedBranchAutoCleanup() functions to detect merged branches against the configured integration branch (dev). This fixes the core bug where 25 worktrees accumulate because they're merged to dev but cleanup only checks main/master.
acceptanceCriteria: Worktree cleanup detects branches merged to dev (not just main/master)
Branch cleanup detects branches merged to dev (not just main/master)
Settings service integration branch is read from gitWorkflow.prBaseBranch
Backward compatibility maintained for repos without gitWorkflow settings

phases.name: add-branch-configuration-validation
phases.title: Add branch configuration validation
phases.description: Add validation and fallback logic for integration branch detection. If gitWorkflow.prBaseBranch is not set or the branch doesn't exist, fall back to main/master detection with logging. Add health checks to verify configured branches exist and warn if integration branch config is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: Graceful fallback to main/master if prBaseBranch not configured
Warning logged when fallback is used
Health check validates configured integration branch exists
Error handling for non-existent configured branches

milestones.title: Standardize Maintenance Configuration
milestones.description: Create a unified maintenance configuration system that allows per-project customization of task schedules, thresholds, and behaviors while maintaining sensible defaults.

phases.name: create-maintenance-settings-schema
phases.title: Create maintenance settings schema
phases.description: Define MaintenanceSettings interface in project-settings.ts with configurable schedules, thresholds, and feature flags for each maintenance task. Include options for worktree retention days, stale feature hours, auto-merge eligibility rules, and task enable/disable flags. Design schema for both global defaults and per-project overrides.
acceptanceCriteria: MaintenanceSettings interface defines all configurable options
TypeScript types for all schedule formats (cron expressions)

phases.name: implement-settings-driven-maintenance-tasks
phases.title: Implement settings-driven maintenance tasks
phases.description: Refactor maintenance-tasks.ts to read configuration from the new MaintenanceSettings instead of hardcoded values. Make task schedules, thresholds, and behaviors configurable through settings. Update AutomationService to apply maintenance settings when creating built-in automation records.
acceptanceCriteria: All hardcoded thresholds replaced with settings values
Task schedules read from maintenance settings
Settings changes apply without server restart

phases.name: add-maintenance-settings-ui
phases.title: Add maintenance settings UI
phases.description: Create maintenance settings panel in the Settings view to allow runtime configuration of maintenance tasks. Include controls for task schedules, thresholds, integration branch setting, and individual task enable/disable toggles. Add validation for cron expressions and threshold values.
acceptanceCriteria: Maintenance settings panel accessible in Settings
Real-time settings update without server restart

milestones.title: Remove Temporary Stop-gaps
milestones.description: Identify and remove all temporary maintenance solutions that were meant as quick fixes. Replace them with proper implementations using the new standardized configuration system.

phases.name: audit-temporary-maintenance-solutions
phases.title: Audit temporary maintenance solutions
phases.description: Search codebase for TODO comments, FIXME markers, and 'temporary' references in maintenance-related code. Document all temporary solutions, their original purpose, current impact, and recommended permanent fix. Create migration plan for each identified stop-gap.
acceptanceCriteria: Complete inventory of temporary maintenance solutions
Priority ranking based on technical debt impact

phases.name: replace-hardcoded-maintenance-intervals
phases.title: Replace hardcoded maintenance intervals
phases.description: Replace all hardcoded cron expressions and timing constants in maintenance tasks with settings-driven values. Remove magic numbers for thresholds (2 hours for stale features, 5 minutes for integrity checks, etc.) and make them configurable through the maintenance settings system.
acceptanceCriteria: No hardcoded cron expressions in maintenance code
All timing thresholds configurable via settings
Default values preserved for backward compatibility

phases.name: consolidate-duplicate-branch-detection-logic
phases.title: Consolidate duplicate branch detection logic
phases.description: Extract common branch detection logic (main vs master, integration branch discovery) into a shared utility function. Remove duplicated code between worktree cleanup and branch cleanup functions. Create a centralized git branch helper that all maintenance tasks can use.
acceptanceCriteria: Single source of truth for branch detection logic
Shared utility handles integration branch configuration
All maintenance tasks use common branch detection

milestones.title: Unified Maintenance Framework
milestones.description: Create a scalable maintenance framework that supports plugin-style task registration, consistent error handling, observability, and easy addition of new maintenance tasks.

phases.name: design-maintenance-task-registry
phases.title: Design maintenance task registry
phases.description: Create a MaintenanceTaskRegistry that allows dynamic registration of maintenance tasks with metadata (schedule, dependencies, configuration schema). Define MaintenanceTask interface for consistent task implementation. Support task discovery, dependency resolution, and runtime enable/disable.
acceptanceCriteria: Task metadata includes schedule and dependencies

phases.name: implement-maintenance-task-observability
phases.title: Implement maintenance task observability
phases.description: Add comprehensive observability to maintenance tasks with execution metrics, duration tracking, success/failure rates, and OpenTelemetry tracing. Create maintenance task dashboard data and health endpoints for monitoring task performance and detecting issues.

phases.name: create-maintenance-task-plugin-system
phases.title: Create maintenance task plugin system
phases.description: Enable external plugins to register custom maintenance tasks through the registry. Support task configuration schema validation, lifecycle hooks (pre/post execution), and plugin isolation. Add documentation and examples for creating maintenance task plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: External plugins can register maintenance tasks
Configuration schema validation for plugin tasks
MCP tools for managing plugin tasks

researchSummary: The current maintenance system has several issues: 1) Worktree cleanup bug where tasks only check main/master instead of the dev integration branch, causing 25 stale worktrees; 2) Hardcoded schedules and thresholds scattered throughout maintenance-tasks.ts; 3) Duplicate branch detection logic; 4) No unified configuration system; 5) Limited observability for maintenance task performance; 6) No plugin system for custom maintenance tasks. The system needs modernization to support the team's dev-first workflow and enable scalable maintenance task management.

prd.situation: protoLabs Studio's maintenance system was built incrementally with hardcoded assumptions about git workflow (main/master branches) and fixed schedules. The team now uses dev as the integration branch, but maintenance tasks still check main/master for merged branches, causing worktree accumulation. Additionally, there's no unified configuration system, making it difficult to customize maintenance behavior per project or adjust schedules without code changes.
prd.problem: The maintenance system has four key issues: 1) Integration branch awareness - worktree cleanup only checks main/master, not dev, causing 25 stale worktrees for merged features; 2) Configuration inflexibility - schedules and thresholds are hardcoded in maintenance-tasks.ts; 3) Code duplication - branch detection logic is repeated across multiple functions; 4) Limited extensibility - no framework for adding new maintenance tasks or monitoring their performance.
prd.approach: Create a comprehensive maintenance system overhaul in four phases: 1) Fix integration branch awareness by making maintenance tasks read gitWorkflow.prBaseBranch instead of assuming main/master; 2) Create a standardized MaintenanceSettings configuration system with UI controls; 3) Remove temporary stop-gaps and consolidate duplicate logic; 4) Build a unified maintenance framework with plugin support, observability, and dynamic task registration.
prd.results: A modern, configurable maintenance system that: correctly detects merged branches against the dev integration branch; provides UI controls for all maintenance schedules and thresholds; eliminates code duplication through shared utilities; supports plugin-style task registration; includes comprehensive observability and health monitoring; scales with team workflow changes without requiring code modifications.
constraints: Maintain backward compatibility for existing maintenance task behavior
Settings changes must apply without server restart where possible
All maintenance tasks must continue running during the migration
Plugin system must provide proper isolation and error boundaries
Performance impact must be minimal - maintenance tasks are background processes
